title: Eva Store reopened
wikitext:
[]: Eva Store reopened             Quote Post by vito » Sun Oct 05, 2014 11:30 am Didn't see a thread for it, personally I think the new place is rather lame http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-3311.html http://weekly.ascii.jp/elem/000/000/263/263364/ http://news.mynavi.jp/articles/2014/10/03/eva/ http://eva-gate.jp/index.php?mmmsid=evaml&actype=report_view&report_id=report_360&obj_id=report_360&seq_id=360&category=report

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Sun Oct 05, 2014 2:56 pm Man, that's a really bland store. Now I'm really happy I've managed to see the cool one in Harajuku! Why did they close it down anyways?

[]:             Quote Post by GT-One » Sun Oct 05, 2014 4:16 pm And here I am, thinking I should probably have visited the store in Harajuku at least once during my first two trips to Japan - that is, if I also knew, the store existed back then. Well, I guess you can't help it, so let's head to Ikebukuro now!

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Sun Oct 05, 2014 8:08 pm I will be hitting the new place up in 2 weeks and yeah, I am disappointed that it is smaller and looks to be half of the size of what would have been a floor in the previous store. Some consolation is that I will also be hitting up Eva-Ya in Hakone later in my trip. No one here has yet said they have been to Hakone.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Sun Oct 05, 2014 9:59 pm I've been to Hakone. Just sayin'.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Mon Oct 06, 2014 12:09 pm How was it?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Oct 06, 2014 12:29 pm Part of me is desperate to go. But the more sensible part of me knows that I would go broke no matter how much money I took in with me, so it's probably best if I never get the chance.

[]:             Quote Post by vito » Mon Oct 06, 2014 3:41 pm Mostly merch shots: http://blog.goo.ne.jp/kirapika05/e/42c91388e1404576d67c07e05031d0df

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Tue Oct 07, 2014 7:18 pm Bagheera wrote: Part of me is desperate to go. But the more sensible part of me knows that I would go broke no matter how much money I took in with me, so it's probably best if I never get the chance.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Oct 07, 2014 7:19 pm AR-99 wrote: Would you consider a trip to the Evastore a bucket list item?

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Tue Oct 07, 2014 7:33 pm You mean the Wunder flight jacket that Asuka's wearing? That is a cool 63,720円. Another issue is size - for shirts I've found the equivalents are usually a size lower than American, i.e. a JP L = a US M, but something like this could be dicey. The black is cool but the text is more muted on the green and I think having the back milspec text in stenciling in white and so visible is silly. Planning to target the smaller items, 3rd anniversary merch, and food within reason.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Oct 07, 2014 7:59 pm Fuck it, I'd buy it. U.S. 2xl (these days I'm a XL U.S.). And green, all the way.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Tue Oct 07, 2014 8:13 pm Let's take this to PM for further discussion.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Mon Feb 09, 2015 11:15 pm My partner just put a new post on her blog about the Eva store Ikebukuro location: http://taylortotokyo.com/2015/02/10/evangelion-store-ikebukuro/ Photos were taken in November last year... and that's my backside in one of them!

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Feb 10, 2015 4:31 pm Sailor Moon had a Christmas display on the floor below, I couldn't help but find that funny.
summary: []: Eva Store reopened             Quote Post by vito » Sun Oct 05, 2014 11:30 am Didn't see a thread for it, personally I think the new place is rather lame http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-3311.html http://weekly.ascii.jp/elem/000/000/263/263364/ http://news.mynavi.jp/articles/2014/10/03/eva/ http://eva-gate.jp/index.php?mmmsid=evaml&actype=report_view&report_id=report_360&obj_id=report_360&seq_id=360&categ
